QF.id: Terminology in Equity and Debt Markets_15

question.text: A callable bond gives the right to:

answer.text: The bondholder to demand early repayment
The issuer to redeem the bond before maturity
The bondholder to convert into equity
The regulator to cancel the bond

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A callable bond gives the issuer (not the holder) the right to redeem (call back) the bond before its stated maturity, usually at a specified call price. **The Trap:** A puttable bond gives the holder the right to demand early repayment. A convertible bond allows conversion to equity. Callable is specifically the issuer's right. **Cross-Exam Context:** Bond features (callable, puttable, convertible) are tested in NISM Series XV and IV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Issuers call bonds when interest rates fall — they repay expensive old debt and refinance at lower rates. This limits bondholder upside.
